seo_title: FAST-GOAL: Fast and Efficient Global-local Object Alignment Learning
meta_description: Improving vision-language models' ability to handle lengthy text descriptions with FAST-GOAL, a fine-tuning method that enhances global-local semantic…
body_html:
What is FAST-GOAL? FAST-GOAL (Fast and Efficient Global-local Object Alignment Learning) is a fine-tuning method that enhances the ability of vision-language models to handle lengthy text descriptions through global-local semantic alignment.

Key ComponentsFast Local Image-Sentence Matching (FLISM): efficiently extracts local image regions through object detection and spatial division, then matches them with corresponding sentences.Token Similarity-based Learning (TSL): maximizes the similarity between patch tokens from specific regions in the image and their corresponding region embeddings, applying the same principle to text.Dataset IntroductionGLIT100k: a dataset that provides both global image-lengthy caption pairs and context-derived local pairs, where local descriptions are extracted from global captions to maintain semantic coherence.Experimental ResultsDatasetImprovement over BaselineDOCCI35.6% improvementDCI28.5% improvementMSCOCO21.1% improvementFlickr30k19.3% improvement

source.title: FAST-GOAL: Fast and Efficient Global-local Object Alignment Learning
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called FAST-GOAL, which enhances the ability of vision-language models like CLIP to handle lengthy text descriptions by aligning images and text more efficiently. The method uses two key components: Fast Local Image-Sentence Matching and Token Similarity-based Learning to extract local image regions and match them with corresponding sentences. Through experiments on various datasets, FAST-GOAL achieves significant improvements over baselines, enabling effective adaptation of CLIP to detailed textual descriptions while maintaining computational efficiency.
